semanticCore: Hating, detesting, frying, dismissing, tossing, or light
quranicRelevance: God not hating the Prophet (Qala - 'ma qala'), and hating bad deeds ('al-qaleena').

keyDerivations.meaning: To hate, detest, loathe, abandon in anger, or dismiss
Haters, detesters, or those who loathe/reject actions

figurativeOrRareUsages: مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ (93:3): Your Lord has not abandoned you, nor has He detested/hated (you) (consolation during revelation pause).
discardedNoise: Frying fish in hot oil (Qalay) - culinary manuals.
summaryEn: The root represents intense hatred and abandonment, comforting the Prophet during a pause in revelation by declaring that God neither abandoned nor hated (Qala) him.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر البغض الشديد والهجر غضباً (قلى)، مسلياً فؤاد النبي بعد انقطاع الوحي بأن ربه ما هجره وما أبغضه (وما قلى)، ومقرراً كره المعاصي.
rawText: قلى 1 : see 4; and ثَمَّهُ, in two places.
